metadata.primary_method: PERT three-point with RCF-anchored M values
metadata.monte_carlo_skip_reason: N=3 session events, insufficient throughput history for meaningful Monte Carlo simulation. Applicable after 8+ weeks of per-TSK granular events.
metadata.pricing_model: claude-opus-4-8 @ $5.00/1M input, $25.00/1M output, $0.50/1M cache_read, $6.25/1M cache_write (pricing.yaml valid_from 2026-01-01)

source: session:f10584c7 (2026-06-04, soli-factory, $82.45, partial docs + framework work)
session:2dcc6e02 (2026-06-08, soli-factory, $85.95, 39 TSK docs milestone — RUN-REPORT cites ~$201 total)
reference_class_quality.similarity_rationale: Same factory (soli-multi-agents-factory), same agent type (docs-dev), same code_path ('.'), same layer (docs), same .claude/ file tree scope. Minor difference: Sprint 20 includes TSK-136 L (new bash scripts) which is marginally heavier than pure-text edits in v2.19 milestone.
reference_class_quality.confidence_derivation: N=3 -> bucket=low (1-9 range, ADR-025 §A-C). similarity=high -> upgrade +1 -> medium. Floor never goes below very_low.
reference_class_quality.warning: N=3 is below the medium threshold (10-29). The upgrade to medium is justified by high similarity but remains fragile. Per-TSK granular events not available: the 3 data points are session-level aggregates (full sessions, not per-TSK). Dividing session cost by TSK count introduces systematic undercount (session overhead diluted across TSK divisor). The PERT P-values mitigate this via pessimistic scenario. Bias of optimism partially mitigated (Kahneman/Flyvbjerg) but not eliminated with N=3.

reference_class_derivation.note: The 2026-06-08 session cost of $85.95 (confirmed via pricing.yaml rates applied to event tokens) covers 39 TSK docs + orchestration overhead + final lint + analytics instrumentation. Adjusting to 70% pure-dev execution: $60.2 / 39 TSK = $1.54/TSK. However, the full session divides $85.95 / 39 = $2.20/TSK. Using $3.61/TSK (adjusted from 3-session total ~$201 / 39 at 70% dev fraction) as PERT M anchor for medium-size TSK. Cross-check anchor: $2.20/TSK (raw). The two anchors produce a P50 range of $18-$40; central estimate $29.

pert_decomposition.anchor_source: adjusted reference class (3-session total $200.98, 70% pure-dev, 39 TSK)

tasks.title: Fail-loud Step 1 ux-ui-review-protocol (ADR-063 §A)
tasks.rationale_P: Worst-case: lint failure forces re-run, Step 1 edit triggers cross-ref check with ADR-063 §C, 2x tool calls.

tasks.title: Guard evidence-provenance Step 5 ux-ui-review-protocol (ADR-063 §B)
tasks.rationale_P: Worst-case: schema update triggers review of all finding references in related docs, multiple iterations on evidence field definition.

tasks.title: ux-ui-reviewer.md: Read/Grep + STOP-condition §A (ADR-063 §C)
tasks.rationale_P: Worst-case: frontmatter tools list update triggers lint check on agent schema, minor re-run.

tasks.title: Backing eseguibile reale 3 tool visivi (ADR-063 §D)
tasks.rationale_P: Worst-case: Playwright dependency check requires iterative scripting, check_design_system_conformance.sh parsing logic needs refinement, agent registration update triggers cross-reference fix in skill. 3 new scripts from scratch — structurally different from pure text edits. P=8x anchor justified.
tasks.note: TSK-136 is the dominant cost driver (~35% of sprint P50). High variance due to Playwright deps uncertainty and script logic complexity.

tasks.title: lint-checks.md: Check evidence-provenance (ADR-063 §B)
tasks.rationale_P: Worst-case: determining correct Check numbering sequence requires reading multiple sections of lint-checks.md, minor rework on check definition wording.

tasks.title: Aggiornare ADR-020 + nota PATTERN (ADR-063 §Conseguenze)
tasks.rationale_P: Worst-case: PATTERN.md section references need careful scope check before adding note, minor iteration.

tasks.title: (Opz) Rivalutare model ux-ui-reviewer (ADR-063 §E)
tasks.rationale_P: Worst-case: evaluation reveals Scenario B (model upgrade), requiring justification note + config update.

tasks.title: (micro) cross-ref ADR-032 §B ↔ ADR-062
tasks.rationale_P: Worst-case: ADR-032 frontmatter format needs review to add related field correctly.

agentic_cost.note: P50=E_PERT=$40.23. P85=E+1.036*sigma=$45.01. P95=E+1.645*sigma=$47.82. Wide band declared: low=$18 (raw-session-scale, optimistic cache), likely=$29 (midpoint of two anchors), high=$45 (PERT P85). Uncertainty band $18-$45 reflects the dual-anchor uncertainty from session-level (not per-TSK granular) reference class. Actual cost may converge to low bound if sprint runs in one high-cache session (70%+ cache_read ratio observed in reference sessions).

cross_check.note: Raw session anchor: $2.20/TSK * 8.0 weight units = $17.63. This is the floor estimate assuming same cache behavior and no overhead adjustment.

breakdown.TSK-139_model_eval: 0.5h — TSK-139 is explicitly decision-gated (Scenario A vs B model choice)
breakdown.TSK-138_adr_review: 0.25h — ADR-020 amendment review gate before merge
breakdown.sprint_oversight: 0.75h — wave approvals, parallel_gate_threshold gate, final lint review

split.rationale: 8 TSK consumer:agent (docs-dev riflessivo). Human time limited to: TSK-139 model decision gate (genuinely non-delegable judgement call), TSK-138 ADR-020 amendment review, sprint oversight. Agentic fraction dominant because all 8 TSK are docs-layer with clear DoD and no external system calls (except TSK-136 Playwright which is a script, not an interactive decision).

assumptions: model: claude-opus-4-8 (pricing.yaml valid_from 2026-01-01: $5.00/1M input, $25.00/1M output, $0.50/1M cache_read, $6.25/1M cache_write). This is the model observed in all reference-class sessions. If model changes (e.g., sonnet-4-6 for some agents), agentic cost drops ~40%.

cache_hit_ratio: ~60-70% cache_read (observed in reference sessions: session:2dcc6e02 cache_read=101M vs input=130K, ratio ~780:1 — extreme compression layer). Sprint 20 operates on same codebase: similar cache behavior expected.
iteration_count: 1 review/lint pass per TSK (no CQRL in scope; docs-dev riflessivo does self-lint inline). If lint reveals cross-file inconsistencies, TSK-133/134 may require a second pass.

TSK-136_scope: 3 bash scripts from scratch (capture_screenshot.sh, extract_design_tokens.sh, check_design_system_conformance.sh) + agent registration update. Script logic is straightforward (set -euo pipefail + dep check + main logic). Playwright wrapper for capture_screenshot is the riskiest sub-task (external dep). If Playwright stub is acceptable (fail-loud on missing dep, no real implementation), P converges toward M.
TSK-139_optional: P3 priority; if skipped, sprint total P50 drops to ~$37.89 (remove $2.34). Included in main estimate as declared optional.

no_external_API_calls: no Figma API, no GitHub publisher, no Playwright runtime execution during docs-dev sprint. Tool calls are Read/Edit/Write/Bash (file ops only). Zero tool pricing overhead (tools cost $0 per pricing.yaml).

sprint_execution_mode: parallel within same session (wave dispatch, scheduler.enabled=true). TSK-138 depends on TSK-133+TSK-135 per frontmatter. All others can run in parallel. Single session expected (no multi-session overhead).
estimate.contingency_basis: N=3 sessions (medium confidence after high-similarity upgrade). ADR-025 §C: medium -> contingency 15-25%. Using 20% as central value. Rationale: (1) N=3 is the low end of medium confidence -> conservatism warranted; (2) TSK-136 L has high intrinsic variance (sigma=$3.91, ~28% of sprint sigma) — Playwright scripting uncertainty; (3) session-level reference class (not per-TSK granular) introduces systematic estimation error. Reduced from 35% (EST-2026-06-08-001 N=0) because N>0 now provides empirical anchor.

budget_prudente.note: Planning ceiling for sprint agentic cost. Includes P85 scenario + 20% buffer. Does not include human cost (EUR, separate budget line).

sensitivity_drivers.impact: +$14.73 vs P50 if worst-case (P=$28.86 vs E=$14.13). Represents 55% of sprint variance.
sensitivity_drivers.description: TSK-136 is the single largest cost driver: creating 3 bash scripts including a Playwright wrapper. If Playwright scripting requires iterative debugging or the check_design_system_conformance.sh grep/awk logic needs refinement, cost spikes. This TSK has P/O ratio of 5.3 (highest in sprint).
sensitivity_drivers.mitigation: Scope capture_screenshot.sh as a thin wrapper (fail-loud on missing Playwright, no real browser launch in sprint context). The DoD requires fail-loud on dep missing, not a working Playwright integration. This brings M->O, collapsing variance.

sensitivity_drivers.description: The reference class is session-level, not per-TSK. Dividing $201/39 TSK includes orchestration overhead, lint runs, premortem, analytics instrumentation — work not in Sprint 20 scope. The overhead adjustment (70% pure-dev) is an assumption, not a measurement. If overhead was actually 50% of session (not 30%), the per-TSK anchor rises to $5.15 and sprint P50 climbs to ~$57.
sensitivity_drivers.mitigation: After Sprint 20 completes, add per-TSK granular events to analytics/events/. This directly calibrates the reference class for Sprint 21 onwards.

sensitivity_drivers.impact: -30% to -50% on agentic cost if cache_read stays at 780:1 ratio (as observed in reference sessions).
sensitivity_drivers.description: The reference sessions show extreme cache_read ratios (101M cache_read vs 130K input for session:2dcc6e02). Sprint 20 operates on the same codebase; if the cache remains warm, effective cost per token is dominated by cache_read ($0.50/1M vs $5.00/1M input). This is already baked into the reference class but worth declaring explicitly.
sensitivity_drivers.mitigation: Run Sprint 20 within a single session to maximize cache reuse. Cache warm-up cost is amortized over all 8 TSK.

reference_class_quality.warning: N=3 (low bucket, upgraded to medium for high similarity). Reference class is session-level aggregate, not per-TSK granular. Per-TSK variation within sessions is unobservable from current data. Wide band ($18-$45 P50) reflects this limitation. NOT a very_low confidence (N>0, data exists), but NOT a high confidence either (N<10, no per-TSK breakdown). Flyvbjerg bias partially mitigated by empirical anchor but optimism bias residual.

notes: STIMA, NON COMMITMENT. P50=$40.23 non e' una deadline. P85=$45.01 e' la soglia per pianificazione prudente (EP-010 invariante: una stima e' una distribuzione, non un impegno contrattuale).

Primo estimate post-EP-013: per la prima volta la reference class non e' N=0 (cfr EST-2026-06-08-001 dove N=0 era il finding principale di dogfooding). N=3 e' ancora basso ma l'esistenza di dati reali riduce il bias strutturale vs pure PERT-elicitation.

Wide band dichiarata: $18-$45 (low/likely/high). La banda larga riflette la granularita' session-level (non per-TSK) dei dati di riferimento. Non e' falsa precisione: e' onesta dichiarazione del limite del dato.

TSK-136 L domina: ~35% del P50 e ~55% della varianza totale. Il range di questo singolo TSK ($5.41-$28.86) e' quasi pari all'intero range degli altri 7 TSK combinati. Gestire TSK-136 come task ad alto rischio individuale.
Monte Carlo non disponibile: N=3 sessioni, nessun dato per-TSK granulare. Non applicabile per throughput forecast.

Raccomandazione: dopo Sprint 20, registrare gli eventi per-TSK in analytics/events/. Questo ridurra' la banda di incertezza del 60-70% per Sprint 21 (N passera' da 3 sessioni a 8+ task individuali, con breakout per size).
